Package neqsim.process.equipment.electrolyzer

Class ElectrolyzerIVCharacteristic

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.electrolyzer.ElectrolyzerIVCharacteristic
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ElectrolyzerIVCharacteristic extends Object implements Serializable
Simple I-V (polarisation) characteristic for water electrolyzers.

Computes the cell voltage from current density as the sum of three terms:

  • Reversible voltage from the Nernst equation, temperature-corrected from 1.229 V at 25 °C using dE_rev/dT = -0.85 mV/K (Larminie & Dicks, Fuel Cell Systems Explained, 2nd ed., 2003, Ch. 2).
  • Activation overpotential from a Tafel form A &middot; ln(j / j0) with technology-specific Tafel slope A and exchange current density j0.
  • Ohmic + diffusion losses from an effective area-specific resistance R multiplied by current density.

For a textbook PEM stack at 80 °C and 2 A/cm² this yields about 1.85 V (Carmo et al., Int. J. Hydrogen Energy 38 (2013) 4901-4934). For alkaline at 80 °C and 0.4 A/cm² it yields about 1.85 V (Ursua et al., Proc. IEEE 100 (2012) 410-426). The model is deliberately a coarse engineering correlation, not a CFD-resolved electrochemistry model.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim contributors

  • Constructor Details

    • ElectrolyzerIVCharacteristic

      public ElectrolyzerIVCharacteristic(ElectrolyzerTechnology technology)
      Create an I-V characteristic with technology-specific defaults.

      Default coefficients (Tafel slope, exchange current density, area-specific resistance) are picked to reproduce the cell voltage in ElectrolyzerTechnology at the technology's nominal current density and temperature within ~3%.

      Parameters:
      technology - electrolyzer technology

  • Method Details

    • setTafelSlope

      public void setTafelSlope(double tafelSlope)
      Set Tafel slope used in eta_act = A &middot; log10(j/j0) (V/decade).
      Parameters:
      tafelSlope - Tafel slope (V/decade), must be positive

    • getTafelSlope

      public double getTafelSlope()
      Get the Tafel slope.
      Returns:
      Tafel slope (V/decade)

    • setExchangeCurrentDensity

      public void setExchangeCurrentDensity(double exchangeCurrentDensity)
      Set exchange current density j0 (A/cm2). Must be positive.
      Parameters:
      exchangeCurrentDensity - exchange current density (A/cm2)

    • getExchangeCurrentDensity

      public double getExchangeCurrentDensity()
      Get the exchange current density.
      Returns:
      exchange current density (A/cm2)

    • setAreaSpecificResistance

      public void setAreaSpecificResistance(double areaSpecificResistance)
      Set the area-specific resistance (ohm*cm2). Must be non-negative.
      Parameters:
      areaSpecificResistance - area-specific resistance (ohm*cm2)

    • getAreaSpecificResistance

      public double getAreaSpecificResistance()
      Get the area-specific resistance.
      Returns:
      area-specific resistance (ohm*cm2)

    • getTechnology

      public ElectrolyzerTechnology getTechnology()
      Get the electrolyzer technology these coefficients were configured for.
      Returns:
      technology

    • getReversibleVoltage

      public double getReversibleVoltage(double temperatureK)
      Get the reversible (Nernst) voltage at the given temperature.
      Parameters:
      temperatureK - stack temperature (Kelvin), must be positive
      Returns:
      reversible cell voltage (V)

    • getCellVoltage

      public double getCellVoltage(double currentDensity, double temperatureK)
      Get the cell voltage at the given current density and temperature.

      E_cell = E_rev(T) + A &middot; log10(j / j0) + R &middot; j

      Returns the reversible voltage if currentDensity is at or below j0 (the cell is operating below the activation threshold).

      Parameters:
      currentDensity - current density (A/cm2), must be non-negative
      temperatureK - stack temperature (Kelvin), must be positive
      Returns:
      cell voltage (V)
